mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals improving household demand and upward revisions to near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value at the decision point was 339299.0
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of observations or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the federal funds rate raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions faster than earnings expectations adjust, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.50 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value at the decision point was 2.63
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment is rising and unemployment is falling, labor income growth supports consumption without signaling recession, increasing expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE falls by at least 0.1 percentage point over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value at the decision point was 132926.0 and UNRATE was 5.2
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition has no positive return premium versus all other months, or if its 20-day hit rate is below 50% in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weakening real activity and lower operating leverage for cyclical firms, causing negative revisions to earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 0.5% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value at the decision point was 118.527
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not statistically below zero at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Very high housing starts combined with rising short rates signals a late housing-cycle squeeze in which rate-sensitive demand is vulnerable to reversal, reducing growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 2000 and FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value at the decision point was 2195.0 and FEDFUNDS was 2.63
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST-high and FEDFUNDS-rising interaction does not underperform other periods over 60 trading days, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer prices increases the probability of additional policy tightening and lowers the present value of future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds its 12-month inflation rate by more than 1 percentage point
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value at the decision point was 192.0
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the average 5-day return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment paired with strong payroll growth signals tight labor markets and rising wage-pressure risk, which increases expected policy restraint and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 5.5 and PAYEMS rises by more than 300 thousand over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is rising
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value at the decision point was 5.2
failure_condition: Reject if this tight-labor interaction has a nonnegative average 20-day forward return or if it fails to underperform periods with UNRATE above 5.5 by at least 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive real GDP growth indicates broad demand expansion and improving aggregate profit prospects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value at the decision point was 10994.3
failure_condition: Reject if positive year-over-year GDPC1 growth is not associated with positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if its rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 50%.
